compiled_state.title: As a DSO developer, I can maintain sprint skill using primary ticket terminology
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Rename all "epic"-specific terminology in sprint SKILL.md to "primary ticket" and migrate CHECKPOINT schema from positional counters to semantic names.
**Why**: Foundation for accepting any ticket type — all downstream stories depend on the renamed terminology and checkpoint format.
**Scope**:
- IN: Sprint SKILL.md phase name renames, variable reference renames (epic_id → primary_ticket_id), CHECKPOINT migration to semantic names, backward-compatible reading of old positional checkpoints
- OUT: New routing logic, clarity gate, script changes — those are Story 3

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, sprint SKILL.md uses "primary ticket" terminology throughout — all phase names renamed (e.g., "Epic Selection" → "Primary Ticket Selection"), all internal variable references changed from epic_id to primary_ticket_id
  ← Satisfies: "Sprint SKILL.md replaces all epic-specific terminology with primary ticket"
- When this story is complete, CHECKPOINT schema uses semantic names (e.g., CHECKPOINT:batch-3-complete) rather than positional counters, and sprint reads old positional-counter checkpoints without error
  ← Satisfies: "CHECKPOINT schema uses semantic names... Sprint reads old positional-counter checkpoints without error"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] ~50+ "epic" references to rename in sprint SKILL.md — verify no orphaned references remain after rename
- [Scope] The rename must include environment variables (SPRINT_EPIC_ID in sprint-next-batch.sh, issue-batch.sh), Python variables (epic_id in inline Python), agent prompt placeholders (EPIC_ID in red-test-evaluator.md, red-task-escalation.md), and the SKILL.md orchestration context envelope
